creator: takefu@airport.fm
text:
$B$?$1$U!wBg:eK-Cf$G$9!#(B

1) Oversight had been.
2) Sure.

Thanks for patch creation.
No problem with patch.


Frederic Culot $B$5$s$O=q$-$^$7$?(B (2011/12/30 18:08):
> Hi Takefu,
> 
> thank you for submitting this update, however I have a few questions
> about it:
> 
> 1) the vendor specifically requests POE version 1.350 and in your PR
> you mention it as well but it is not specified in the port's Makefile.
> Indeed, p5-POE>=0 is used instead, shouldn't it be p5-POE>=1.350?
> 
> 2) p5-Capture-Tiny usage is made optional in the port's Makefile, but I
> do not see the interest of it. Unless I am wrong, this dependency is
> only used for testing purposes and there is no support for it in
> p5-POE-Quickie itself so I find the OPTIONS description misleading.
> Wouldn't it be better to simplify the port's Makefile and place it in
> a TEST_DEPENDS section?
> 
> I tried to build and test the port using the above suggestions and all
> went well apparently. Here is the patch I used:
> 
> http://people.freebsd.org/~culot/files/p5-POE-Quickie.diff
> 
> and the corresponding tinderbox log:
> 
> http://people.freebsd.org/~culot/tinderbox/9.0-FreeBSD/p5-POE-Quickie-0.18.log
> 
> Running make test in $WRKSRC after installing the ports mentioned in the
> TEST_DEPENDS section did not report any error.
> 
> Takefu, could you please review my patch and tell me if I am doing
> something wrong? If not then I will commit this updated patch.
> 
> Thanks,
> culot@
> 
> 


--
